data.title: Aprea Therapeutics prices $5.6M private placement
data.description:
• Aprea Therapeutics (NASDAQ: APRE) said on Thursday that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with new and existing healthcare-focused institutional investors, as well as certain insiders, to sell an aggregate of 6.29M shares of common stock.
  • The offering also includes warrants to purchase up to 6.29M shares of common stock and is being conducted at-the-market under Nasdaq rules.
  • Each share and accompanying warrant is priced at $0.89. The warrants, exercisable immediately at $0.765, expire two years after the registration statement becomes effective.
  • Net proceeds from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes and research and development expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 30, 2026.
  • Maxim Group is acting as the sole placement agent in connection with the offering.
  • Shares -7.83%.

data.title: SAP's stock falls after cloud backlog miss
data.description:
Shares of SAP (SAP) fell about 16% premarket on Thursday after current cloud backlog in the fourth quarter came in below expectations.

In the fourth quarter, the current cloud backlog grew by 16% to €21.05B and was up 25% at constant currencies. However, CEO Christian Klein had earlier said that SAP was targeting 26% growth, Bloomberg News reported.

The current cloud backlog in the fourth quarter missed SAP’s own target. “If you look at the pipeline, and I’ve seen it many times, I would rather see 25% as a disappointment,” said Klein on the company's third quarter earnings call in October 2025. “26% would be, in my eyes, a great result.”

Klein said in a call with analysts on Thursday that the lower current cloud backlog was partly due to negotiations taking longer. "It’s a reflection of what is happening in the world,” Klein said, adding that clients are asking about potential sanctions, export restrictions, and new regulations. “This is not a reflection of a demand issue,” according to the report.

For the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31, non-IFRS EPS (basic) grew 16% year-over-year to €1.62. Total revenue rose 3% (9% at constant currencies) year-over-year to €9.68.

SAP said Cloud revenue rose 19% to €5.61B and up 26% at constant currencies. Cloud ERP Suite revenue grew 23% to €4.86B and up 30% at constant currencies.

"Q4 was a strong cloud quarter, with bookings resulting in 30% Total Cloud Backlog growth to a record 77 billion Euros. The significant Current Cloud Backlog growth in Q4 has laid a strong foundation for accelerating Total Revenue growth through 2027. SAP Business AI has become a main driver for growth as it was included in two thirds of our Q4 cloud order entry, combined with strong AI adoption across the ERP Suite," said Klein in the company's earnings release on Thursday.

The company noted that as of Dec. 31, 2025, total cloud backlog was up 22% to €77.29B and up 30% at constant currencies.

Cloud revenue for the full year 2025 rose 23% to €21.02B and was up 26% at constant currencies.

Klein said on a call with analysts that the threat to SAP’s business from AI is overstated, as large language models, or LLMs, alone cannot be depended on for some use cases and that the underlying business data SAP has access to is necessary. “To make this very clear, we are winning deals because of AI. We are not losing deals because of AI,” Klein noted, the report added.

Outlook

For 2026, SAP expects €25.8B to €26.2B cloud revenue at constant currencies (2025: €21.02B), up 23% to 25% at constant currencies. This compares to an average analyst estimate of €26B, according to Bloomberg.

SAP noted that constant currency current cloud backlog growth will slightly decelerate in 2026 (2025: 25%).

The company expects constant currency total revenue growth to accelerate through 2027.

Buyback

SAP announced a new share repurchase program worth up to €10B. It is scheduled to start in February and is expected to be completed by the end of 2027.

Wall Street Analysts Commentary

"As usual, results from SAP include some pluses and minuses, with the debates today likely focusing on CCB [current cloud backlog]. Robust guidance, starting to raise the curtain on FY27 guidance and a new share buyback announcement reflect management confidence in the business going forwards. But whether that's enough to reverse some of the worst sentiment we have seen towards software remains to be seen," said Jefferies' analysts led by Charles Brennan.

The analysts noted that more important than the P&L, is the focus on the current cloud backlog which came in at 25%. The analysts added that in prior communications, the company had implied that 25% would be "disappointing" and, as a result, investors are likely to come to the same conclusion.

"However, the statement and conference call noted that total order entry came in ahead ofexpectations, albeit with a higher mix of government deals where termination clauses mean deals don't necessarily appear in CCB and larger deals with cloud ramps in outer years. These factors impacted CCB by 1%," said Brennan and his team.

Update: The story was updated with Jefferies analysts note.

data.title: Nokia targets €2B–€2.5B 2026 operating profit with 6%–8% network infrastructure growth as company ramps AI and optical investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nokia (NOK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Justin Hotard, President and CEO, emphasized, "Overall, our fourth quarter performance was in line with our expectations, reflecting disciplined execution across the business. Net sales grew 3% in the quarter to EUR 6.1 billion, with operating profit of EUR 1 billion and free cash flow of EUR 0.2 billion." He highlighted the strategic acquisition of Infinera, a new operating model, and a sharpened focus on areas offering differentiation and sustainable leadership, especially within Network Infrastructure and AI/cloud-driven opportunities.

  • Hotard noted strong momentum in Network Infrastructure: "In the fourth quarter, net sales grew 7%, driven by optical networks, which grew by 17%. Order intake was solid across both optical and IP networks with a book-to-bill above 1, supported by particularly strong demand from AI and cloud customers." He referenced investments to capture AI supercycle demand and discussed ramping production of 800-gig ZR and ZR+ pluggable products.

  • The CEO described product launches in IP Networks—"We launched two new products in the quarter, the 7220 IXR-H6 switching platform powered by Broadcom's TH6 and our Agentic AI solution for event-driven automation management, which reduces network downtime by 96%. We also secured a design win for our next-generation data center switching platform."

  • In Mobile Infrastructure, Hotard announced the integration of core software, radio networks, and technology standards into a new segment. He cited a 5G core deal with Telia, a collaboration with Bharti Airtel, and a new partnership with NVIDIA. He also referenced the creation of Nokia Defense and the full acquisition of Nokia Shanghai Bell.

  • Marco Wiren, CFO, stated, "Net sales were EUR 6.1 billion, that's up 3% on the prior year. Gross margin was 48.1%, an improvement of 90 basis points, driven by improvements in Mobile Networks and Cloud and Network Services."

Outlook

  • The company is targeting 2026 operating profit between EUR 2 billion and EUR 2.5 billion. Hotard reiterated, "Our first KPI is to deliver 6% to 8% compound annual growth in network infrastructure between 2025 and 2028 on a constant currency and portfolio basis and 10% to 12% in the combined Optical and IP Networks businesses. In 2026, we expect growth rates in both cases to be in line with these long-term targets."

  • Fixed Networks growth will be limited due to product prioritization, but fiber OLT portfolio growth is expected to continue.

  • Network Infrastructure operating margin is expected to expand toward 13% to 17% by 2028, with measured expansion in 2026 from the 9.5% achieved in 2025.

  • Mobile Infrastructure targets at least EUR 1.5 billion in operating profit, consistent with 2025.

  • For Portfolio Businesses, the 2026 target is a lower operating loss than in 2025, when the segment generated EUR 850 million in net sales and a EUR 97 million operating loss.

  • Group Common costs are expected to decrease to EUR 150 million in 2026 from EUR 190 million in 2025.

Financial Results

  • CFO Wiren reported, "Operating margin was 17.3%, and this is 90 basis points below the prior year, impacted primarily by increased investments in growth areas, including the Infinera acquisition. We generated EUR 226 million of free cash flow and ended the quarter with EUR 3.4 billion of net cash."

  • Network Infrastructure net sales grew 7%, with AI and cloud customers accounting for 16% of segment net sales and 30% of Optical Networks. Gross margin declined by 80 basis points to 44.6%.

  • Cloud and Network Services saw a 4% decline in the quarter, but full-year net sales grew 6%. Gross margin increased 650 basis points, benefiting from a provision reversal.

  • Mobile Networks net sales increased 6%, driven by growth in Middle East and Africa, Japan, and Indonesia. Gross margin was 40.1% for the quarter and 37% for the year.

  • Nokia Technologies net sales declined by 17% in the quarter. Operating profit was affected by a EUR 20 million impairment charge.

  • Cash increased due to a EUR 0.9 billion NVIDIA equity investment and the completion of the NSB shares acquisition, impacting cash by EUR 0.5 billion.

Q&A

  • Alexander Duval, Goldman Sachs: Asked about conservatism in Optical guidance and the sub-seasonal Q1 outlook. Hotard responded, "We are still transitioning from a base that was, was still very telco-centric in '25, so 70-30… we're building off that base. We're excited about the order momentum… but balance on where we are today given the starting point that we had."

  • Richard Kramer, Arete: Asked about CapEx visibility and restructuring costs. Hotard said investments are based on long-term trends and confidence in the market. Wiren explained, "We guided by that time that we expect cost savings between EUR 800 million to EUR 1.2 billion, and we said that also the costs to generate these savings will be about the same and also the cash flow is following that."

  • Simon Leopold, Raymond James: Queried about Nokia's position in scale-across optical projects. Hotard described long-term demand drivers in optics and the importance of co-developing with customers.

  • Sami Sarkamies, Danske Bank: Asked about IP Networks growth and CapEx. Hotard responded, "I'm really, really pleased with the design win we had in Q4… But I think this business needs a little bit of time to ramp."

  • Additional questions covered regulatory impacts, supply constraints, North America performance, operating guidance range, memory prices, capital allocation, capacity investments, margin phasing, and cost savings.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on growth visibility, supply constraints, CapEx rationale, and cost structure, reflecting a neutral to slightly cautious stance.

  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, with Hotard reiterating focus, discipline, and optimism regarding AI/cloud and optical growth. The tone remained consistent quarter-over-quarter, with continued emphasis on execution and predictability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language became more specific, with explicit operating profit and margin targets for 2026. Strategic focus sharpened on AI/cloud, Network Infrastructure, and profitability over revenue in Mobile.

  • Analysts in both quarters focused on AI/cloud momentum, supply chain, CapEx, and cost discipline.

  • Key metrics shifted from order and backlog strength in Q3 to explicit growth and margin targets in Q4. Management's confidence remains steady, continuing to cite disciplined execution and capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing supply constraints in optical technology and the need for continued ecosystem investment.

  • Headwinds from North American customer losses in Radio Access Network were noted, with the market otherwise described as stable.

  • Order momentum and production scaling were highlighted as near-term challenges.

  • Regulatory changes in Europe (Cybersecurity Act, Digital Network Act) are seen as both a challenge and opportunity, with Hotard stating, "If you think about the pace at which we've deployed networks in India or even in North America… the network upgrades that are required in Europe are something well within our scope and capability and manufacturing capacity."

Final Takeaway

Nokia management highlighted 2025 as a foundational year for repositioning the company toward long-term value creation, emphasizing disciplined execution, growth in AI and cloud-driven network infrastructure, and a streamlined operating model. The company provided clear financial targets for 2026, focusing on operating profit, margin expansion, and continued investment in optical and IP networks. Management acknowledged ongoing supply constraints and regional challenges but expressed confidence in the company's direction and ability to execute on its strategy, underpinned by strong order momentum and a robust cash position.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Comcast reports mixed Q4, higher broadband losses than expected
data.description:
Media and cable giant Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) lost more broadband customers than expected in the fourth quarter, on top of reporting a top-line miss but a bottom-line beat.

CMCSA stock is marginally down in premarket trading on the Nasdaq on Thursday.

In Q4, domestic broadband customer losses were 181,000, more than the Bloomberg consensus estimate of 168,194 losses, but domestic video customer losses were 245,000, lower than the 264,547 losses estimate, and domestic wireline additions went up by 364,000 compared to 307,000 additions last year.

Studio revenue fell 7.4% to $3.03B, hurt by lower theatrical revenue in Q4, which was due to tougher comparisons against prior-year releases, including Wicked and The Wild Robot, compared to current-quarter titles Wicked: For Good and Black Phone 2, the company said.

Revenue from its streaming service, Peacock, was $1.63B, up 23% from last year but below the estimate of $1.7B.

The ongoing momentum following the opening of Epic Universe in May last year helped boost revenue in the theme park business by 21.9% to $2.89B.

The total media segment revenue rose 5.5% in the quarter to $7.62B, helped in part by higher domestic advertising revenue, which included the positive impact from the launch of the NBA.

Revenue from connectivity and platforms, which brings in the bulk of total revenue, was down 1.1% at $20.24B.

Net income attributable to Comcast for the three months ended December 31 was $2.17B, or $0.60 per share, compared to $4.78B, or $1.24 per share, for the same period last year, weighed down by a nearly $2B rise in total costs and expenses.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company earned $0.84, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.75 per share.

Revenue was up 1.2% to $32.31B but was below the $32.36B estimate.

data.title: ALT5 Sigma sets up to $100M stock buyback program
data.description:
• ALT5 Sigma (ALTS) shares jumped over 9% premarket after its board authorized up to $100M for a stock buyback program below net asset value and of up to 50M shares, representing ~40% of all outstanding shares and 22% of shares on a fully diluted basis.
  • In addition to the stock repurchase program, the board has authorized management to pursue strategic acquisitions of $WLFI tokens in the open market.
  • ALT5 currently holds ~7.3B $WLFI tokens. Based on prevailing market conditions, these holdings represent ~$1.5B in estimated value as of our last quarterly filing with the SEC.

data.title: Initial jobless claims slip slightly in past week, roughly in line
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for the week ended Jan. 24: -1K to 209K vs. 206K consensus and 210K prior (revised from 200K), according to data released by the Department of Labor on Thursday.

Continuing claims for the week ended Jan. 17: 1.827M vs. 1.860M consensus and 1.865M prior (revised from 1.849M).

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: VSE to acquire Precision Aviation for $2.025B in cash and equity
data.description:
VSE (VSEC), a provider of aftermarket distribution and repair services for the aviation industry, on Thursday said it would acquire Precision Aviation Group (PAG) for a total upfront consideration of about $2.025 billion in cash and equity.

The deal consists of $1.75 billion in cash and about $275 million of equity consideration issued to GenNx, VSEC said.

In addition, the company added that the agreement includes up to $125 million in additional contingent earnout consideration, payable in cash or equity consideration at VSE’s sole discretion, based on PAG’s 2026 adjusted EBITDA performance.

VSEC’s shares rose over 2% in premarket trading on Thursday.

Atlanta, Georgia-based PAG, which is a portfolio company of GenNx360 Capital Partners, was founded in 1996. It is a provider of maintenance, repair, and overhaul services, distribution, and supply chain solutions for the aviation sector.

“This transformational transaction is expected to expand our scale significantly, increase our proprietary solutions content, and further strengthen our position as a mission-critical partner to aviation operators worldwide,” said VSEC CEO John Cuomo.

PAG, which serves both commercial and defense end markets, expects to generate about $615 million of adjusted revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025.

Precision Aviation Group’s adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to be immediately accretive to VSE’s consolidated Adjusted EBITDA margin, it said.

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

VSEC also said it expects revenue of about $290M to $304M and operating income of about $27M to $34M for the fourth quarter.

For fiscal year 2025, the company’s revenue is expected to range from about $1,101M to $1,115M, with operating income of about $84M to $91M. Consensus for fourth quarter and FY25 revenue are $283.34M and $1.09B.

data.title: Royal Caribbean sets up 2026 for another “outstanding” year
data.description:
Royal Caribbean (RCL) shares were launched higher in Thursday’s premarket trading as an “outstanding” 2025 is expected to spill into 2026 as the company set its profit guidance comfortably above Wall Street’s expectations.

“WAVE is off to a great start,” said Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty, referring to industry-wide promotions that run from January to March, adding that he expects “another strong year of financial performance with both revenue and earnings growing double digits, expecting to reach our Perfecta goals by 2027.”

To drive the company’s Perfecta Program—a three-year financial initiative to achieve an EPS CAGR of 20% and high-teen ROIC—Royal Caribbean (RCL) has made investments in vacation experiences, new ships, the expansion of its Celebrity river cruises, and five new destinations in 2028.

These efforts, coupled with the strong financial performance in the fourth quarter, led the company to set ambitious goals for 2026, including adjusted earnings in a range of $17.70 to $18.10 per share, translating to a CAGR of 23% and above the consensus estimate of $17.66 per share.

The company also anticipates double-digit revenue growth in 2026, driven by 6.7% higher capacity and yield growth of 2.1% to 4.1%, which includes 30 basis points of headwind from itinerary modifications in China.

For the first quarter, the company anticipates a profit of $3.18 to $3.28 per share versus estimates of $2.91 per share.

For the reported quarter, the cruise operator earned an adjusted profit of $2.80 per share, up 72% from a year ago and in-line with expectations. Adjusted EBITDA increased 34% to $1.48B, driving the adjusted EBITDA margin up 560 basis points to 34.8%.

Total revenue for the final quarter of the year rose 13.3% to $4.26B, slightly below Wall Street’s lofty expectations.

Shares are currently trading nearly 7% higher.
